mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising retail sales indicate broad-based demand expansion that lifts cyclical equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.5 percent confirms sustained manufacturing output growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.5% MoM correlates with negative or flat 20-day equity returns in more than 50% of observed late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically generating positive forward equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through pricing models.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters consistently yield negative or zero 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple decades of data.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth rate exceeds 3.0 percent, it forces aggressive monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL annualized inflation breaches 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm labor market normalization, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal predicts negative 20-day equity returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample months, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: In supply-constrained recovery environments, a divergence where housing starts exceed 1200 thousand units while industrial production remains below 100 index signals demand outstripping supply capacity, triggering inflationary pressure and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST/INDPRO divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of identified supply-constrained episodes, or the signal's direction flips during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
